title: Reproduce the Hepatoplasmataceae collinear analysis in the web app
summary: Build a five-genome linear comparison from GenBank inputs using browser LOSATP and collinear block reduction.
requirements: Enough available memory for five bacterial GenBank records and LOSATP output.
The five GenBank flat files listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: AP027078
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027131
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027133
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: AP027132
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: NZ_CP006932
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file.

quickReproduce.title: Download the Gallery session
quickReproduce.body: Use the Session button in this Gallery entry to save hepatoplasmataceae_collinear.gbdraw-session.json.

media.alt: Gallery header for the Hepatoplasmataceae collinear analysis with the Session action visible.
media.caption: The Gallery entry keeps the finished interactive SVG, downloadable SVG, and session JSON together.

operations.title: Session button for this Gallery entry

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing session button for this gallery entry.
media.caption: Session button for this Gallery entry in the gallery page view.

quickReproduce.title: Open the web app
quickReproduce.body: Open gbdraw.app, then load the saved .gbdraw-session.json from the session controls.

operations.title: Header session controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Load Session.

media.alt: Actual Linear web app header toolbar with Load Session, Save Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows the Load Session control in its real header-toolbar context.

quickReproduce.title: Inspect the restored diagram
quickReproduce.body: The session restores the diagram settings, uploaded GenBank files, cached LOSATP results, and generated interactive SVG state.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing restored generated diagram.
media.caption: Restored generated diagram in the generated preview view.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Linear mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Linear mode before adding inputs.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing mode segmented control.
media.caption: Mode segmented control in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Upload the five GenBank files
manualSteps.body: Keep the records in the same order as the Gallery command so adjacent comparison tracks match the published example.

operations.title: Linear GenBank uploader with file order

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing linear genbank uploader with file order.
media.caption: Linear GenBank uploader with file order in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Use browser LOSAT
manualSteps.body: In Pairwise Comparisons, choose Run LOSAT. Set LOSAT Mode to LOSATP.
items: Threads per run: Auto unless you need to reproduce a fixed thread count

media.alt: Web app Linear mode input panel with Run LOSAT, LOSATP, Collinear blocks, and Orientation plus identity selected.
media.caption: The Linear input panel exposes LOSATP execution settings and the collinear reduction controls before generation.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing pairwise comparisons panel.
media.caption: Pairwise Comparisons panel in the web app session view.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing losat run settings.
media.caption: LOSAT run settings in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Select collinear reduction
manualSteps.body: In LOSATP settings, set blastp mode to Collinear blocks. Use Orientation + identity for Color mode.
manualSteps.note: These settings summarize orthogroup-backed protein anchors as gene-order-preserving ribbons.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing losatp collinear controls.
media.caption: LOSATP collinear controls in the web app session view.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing collinear color mode.
media.caption: Collinear color mode in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set the diagram layout
manualSteps.body: Use the layout and track options that match the Gallery session.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing linear layout controls.
media.caption: Linear layout controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The first LOSATP run can take time; later runs with the same canonical inputs and LOSAT settings can reuse the raw LOSAT cache.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing generate button and processing context.
media.caption: Generate button and processing context in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Check collinear blocks
manualSteps.body: Use the generated SVG preview to inspect the curved block ribbons between adjacent genomes. Orientation + identity colors separate forward and reverse orientation while retaining identity information.

media.alt: Generated Hepatoplasmataceae collinear diagram with a Collinearity block popup open over the interactive SVG.
media.caption: Click a collinearity ribbon in the interactive SVG to inspect the block ID, orientation, covered orthogroups, and source intervals.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing collinear block popup.
media.caption: Collinear block popup in the popup view.

colorRules.title: Choose the qualifier by intent
colorRules.body: Use gene for shared gene names, locus_tag or protein_id for individual genes, and product for broader functional categories.

colorRules.title: Keep value patterns specific
colorRules.body: Specific rule values are treated as matching patterns, so avoid broad words such as protein unless you intentionally want many features to share one color.

colorRules.title: Use feature clicks to discover qualifiers
colorRules.body: After generation, click a CDS and open the qualifier details before deciding which feature type, qualifier, value, color, and legend caption should become a color rule.

colorRules.title: Separate feature colors from block colors
colorRules.body: Feature color rules affect genome features. Collinear block colors are controlled by the LOSATP Collinear Color mode and the comparison color settings.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click a feature
postGenerationEdits.body: Feature clicks show the selected feature, qualifiers, sequence details, and color controls. Use this to verify gene, locus_tag, product, and protein_id values.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing feature popup.
media.caption: Feature popup in the popup view.

postGenerationEdits.title: Edit the legend
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the legend editor to rename, reorder, or remove feature legend entries after the SVG has been generated.

media.alt: Hepatoplasmataceae collinear tutorial operation screenshot showing legend editor.
media.caption: Legend editor in the web app session view.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export the figure
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. Use PNG or PDF when you need quick sharing or manuscript draft assets.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the rendered Preview export toolbar for SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Linear Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The rendered .preview-toolbar crop shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore inputs, settings, generated output, and post-generation SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Save Session.

media.alt: Actual Linear web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows Save Session in the same header toolbar used to load sessions.

losatTips.title: Start with Auto execution
losatTips.body: Auto uses threaded LOSAT only when the browser supports it and the workload justifies it.

losatTips.title: Prefer Safe total threads
losatTips.body: Safe avoids consuming every logical CPU and is the best default when you want the browser to remain responsive.

losatTips.title: Keep Threads per run on Auto
losatTips.body: Auto lets the app balance parallel pair runs and per-run LOSATP threads from the selected total thread budget.

losatTips.title: Use Serial when the browser is constrained
losatTips.body: If threaded execution is unavailable or the tab becomes unresponsive, switch Execution to Serial or lower the thread budget.

losatTips.title: Reuse the cache
losatTips.body: Raw LOSAT results are reused when canonical inputs and LOSAT settings match, so iterative diagram styling is faster after the first run.

troubleshooting.title: Threaded LOSAT is unavailable
troubleshooting.body: Use Auto or Serial execution. Some browser contexts disable SharedArrayBuffer or cross-origin isolation, which prevents threaded WebAssembly.

troubleshooting.title: The first run feels slow
troubleshooting.body: This example runs five genomes through LOSATP in the browser. Wait for the first cache entry before making styling-only changes.

troubleshooting.title: The output is cluttered
troubleshooting.body: Raise Min block genes or identity thresholds to reduce weak blocks, then regenerate.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Confirm input order, Collinear Color mode, Pairwise match style, Align to Center, Separate Strands, Show GC, and Show skew settings.
